entry.title: [Webinar] Welcome Session for New SAP Cloud ALM Customers | APAC
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to one of the below online webinars where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

What to Expect in the Webinar

The SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinar has been designed to ensure that you and your team are set up for success. During the session, we will:

  • Demonstrate the initial login procedures to access your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • Provide guidance on the next steps to use the solution effectively.
  • Highlight essential training resources to support you and your team’s learning journey.
  • Answer any questions you may have during the session.

Your Next Steps

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM onboarding experience, here’s what you need to do:

  1. Share this information with your key stakeholders.

    • Ensure that IT Admins, Project Leads, and Implementation or Operations Partners responsible for SAP Cloud ALM are informed.
    • Don’t forget to forward the webinar invitation to your project implementation partner for their participation.

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Register Now

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

entry.title: Re: Reconciliation account to be chaned to normal gl account
entry.content: Use the Transaction code OAMK to change the status of GLs of asset from Recon to non recon or vice versa

entry.title: Re: PO to BTP - IS Migration : Query parameters in HTTP r...
entry.content: In addition to Helmut's answer, I actually did this, I sent a sample request from PO to Cloud Integration and fetched the CamelHttpQuery to find out the query parameters, so this is the outcome: msgguid=17e552df-20d4-11f0-cb21-0000003a8cde&qos=EOIO&queueid=12345&interface=order&senderParty=XYZ&senderService=ABC, so you need to create a receiver http adapter in Cloud Integration and in the Query parameter of the adapter construct a query accordingly, usually for the values you use properties or headers to be able to dynamically define the query, Alex

entry.title: Re: Changing Quota value in a Quota arrangement not apply...
entry.content:
Thanks Caetano,

I did run Edit >  Base Quantities > Collective Calculation from the menu which actually filled in the Quota base quantity for me, I had expected this to balance the quota arrangement so it would only account for active supply in the calculations going forward, but there still appeared to be a substantial time buffer before the planned orders were generated in the ration specified in the QUOTA fields.  Should I just manually add a balance in the field to ensure that with the allocated quantities the ratio of Allocated + Base matches the QUOTA field?  Also do you know what historical data feeds the allocated quantity?  (This is just out of interest rather than helping fix the issue).

Ultimately I am going to suggest to my user base to delete the quota arrangement and create a new one when changing the percentages as I believe asking them to create balance records in the Base Quantity will never be done correctly.

entry.title: Re: PO to BTP - IS Migration : Query parameters in HTTP r...
entry.content: We have e.g. one common IDOC separator in Integration Suite for all sending SAP Backends - in order to get knowledge about the real sender, we are using a query parameter that we add to RFC Destination. This will reduce the number of iflows to be build.

entry.title: Re: BRF+ in SAP MDG: Unable to get BP Central Data fields...
entry.content:
Hello,

What do you have in the Signature tab? If you add BP_CENTRL through "Add Existing Data Object" button, you can reach the fields.

entry.title: Re: Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!
entry.content: Do I understand you correctly, that I can use Joule in SAP Workzone where various onprem backends are connected. And that Joule can access / interact with these systems, resp. the data? Is there something necessary to implement in the backend system or is it using the standard integration capabilites via cloud connector etc.?

entry.title: Choosing the Right Authorization Method in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm curious to know which authorization methods you prefer when working with the SAP Integration Suite, including OAuth, Basic Authentication, and Client Certificate Authentication. I've noticed that using Client Certificate Authentication can be more complex due to the need to update certificates with all communication partners every 365 days.

Given these considerations, do you have recommendations or best practices to share? Additionally, does SAP provide any guidance on preferred authorization methods for specific scenarios?

Looking forward to hearing your insights!

Thanks!

Kind regards,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: Preview/Print of a Document (Crystal Report) not working
entry.content:
Hi Andy,

from everything you mention it seems that the report was not properly imported. You need to open it in Crystal reports, go to "Database => Set Datasource Location" and ensure you have the correct connection setup for your current database. Connection should be set up like "DRIVER={HDBODBC};SERVERNODE=your_server:30015;DATABASE=your_database".

Optional parameters for the connection string are the DB user and DB user password, but I do not recommend using them as those can cause an error if vaules change.

BR,

Matija

entry.title: How to use MDRO.SetRecurrence() function in Cloud Application Studio?
entry.content:
Hello Community.

I have to develop a solution that programmatically triggers mass data run scheduled to run every 5 minutes.

We have found a library AP.PlatinumEngineering where there is function called MDRO.setRecurrence(). However it requires a parameter startdatetime which is of type AP.Common.GDT:Global_DateTime.

We tried using Context.GetCurrentGlobalDateTime() but it returns BASIS:Global_DateTime.

There is no way to cast or use the data type AP.Common.GDT:Global_DateTime in a BO element or in an ABSL script file.

How can we then use the function MDRO.SetRecurrence()?

entry.title: How to chain HANA SQL input files in a HANA SQL Input file
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to simplify my configuration. I have written several sql scripts to create users, roles, policies, set parameters etc. Now I would like to chain these scripts in an input file for hdbsql. 

The best way on my opinion:

loggin on with <sid>adm 

hdbsql -d SYSTEMDB -u SYSTEM

\i configure_hana.script

Content of configure_hana.script:

\i create_roles.sql;

\i create_users.sql;

\i set_parameters.sql;

hdbsql executes the first script, but stucks after this. There are ; at the end of each line in the sql script. 

I do not want to store a user with admin authorisation in hdbuserstore and I do not want to execute hdbsql with password in command line. Because of this, I would like to logon first and then call the input file. 

I can`t find a syntax to terminate the input file. 

Can you give me a hint to the solution.

Best Regards

Andreas

entry.title: Re: Setup Fund Management in SAP Public Cloud Grow Step b...
entry.content:
Hello @iRzSAP 

Thank you for your follow-up.

Since you're already working within the SAP Public Cloud Grow environment and have access to the starter system, here’s how to proceed using SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM:

 

Where to Find the Process in Signavio:

You can access Fund Management process content via SAP Signavio Process Navigator:

 SAP Signavio Process Navigator

  • Search for: "Fund Management" or "Budget Availability Control"
  • Filter by Line of Business: Finance and Solution Scenario: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Getting Started – Step-by-Step:

  1. Preparation Phase
    • Define your Fund and Commitment Items.
    • Identify budgeting requirements and structure.
  2. Master Data Setup
    • Use the app “Manage Commitment Items”
    • Maintain Fund, Funded Program, Functional Area, and Funds Center.
  3. Configuration (via SSCUIs)
    • Access via Manage Your Solution → Configure Your Solution.
    • SSCUI examples:
      • 104662 – Activate Budget Availability Control
      • 104666 – Assign Commitment Items
  4. Integration & Testing
    • Map FM objects to controlling and FI.
    • Test with purchase requisitions/invoices to simulate budget checks.
    • Monitor via the Budget Availability Control Monitor app.

Let me know if you need further information.

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: How to turn off the 'Add Comments | Refactor' automatically appearing line in the BAS Editor?
entry.content:
Hi Colleagues,

I'm using BAS, and whenever i select a variable/line in the code editor, automatically an 'Add Comment | Refactor' option appears above the selection, right in the middle of the code, which pushes the entire code down by a line, which, to be diplomatic, is extremely annoying.

I tried to turn off any AI Assistant related settings but could not figure out, how to get rid of these automatically appearing in line 'helping' features.

Do any of you have any ideas? The feature i'm talking about is this:

Every time you select something, this appear above, which moves the entire code.

Thank You and Best Regards,
Mihaly

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI Quartz Theme Issue
entry.content:
There are numerous fixes for Quartz  theme in later patches.

Please test one user with patch 12 and advise if this solves the issue.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Graphical View Filter WHERE IN
entry.content:
Actually I think I am not sure if I understand the question right but you absolutly can do this. When you put a filter operator in a graphical view you can do it

 

[Field to filter]  in ( Select [Field to Filter] from [View] )

Sadly I 've just changed company and I do not have access to Datasphere for the moment  to give you a proper answer but it is totally possible you just have to play with the syntax a little ( I had to use it also)

entry.title: Re: I need to copy functionality to the ASR cockpit
entry.content: -UPDATE- I managed in getting the new value in the table with assigned actions. Next step is to actually make the button work.. I did this by enhancing class /scmtms/cl_ui_tbi_tree, method get_definition. I suspect I might also need to edit the get_data method.

entry.title: Re: Choosing the Right Authorization Method in SAP Integr...
entry.content:
Hello Stephan,

SAP generally advises:

Use OAuth 2.0 whenever supported by the external system — especially for public APIs or cloud apps.

Use Client Certificates when strong mutual authentication is required and both parties support it, typically in B2B or On-Premise scenarios.

Use Basic Auth only when other options are not possible, and only over HTTPS with frequent password rotation.

Hope this helps!

Thanks,

Punith Oswal

entry.title: Re: Hide columns in Overall Release of Purchase Requisiti...
entry.content:
Hi,

as metadata extensions are not allowed for this particular CDS view, UI adaptations cannot be done in the backend model. Have you tried to use Runtime Adaptation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/UI_ADD-ON_FOR_SAP_NETWEAVER_20/17ae0e97e0fc424a9c368f350c0ba6bd/5c424437bf794f809087fdce391149f2.html

Thank you and best regards

Fabian

entry.title: How can we see the commercial metrics from the instance level?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Based on the demo video - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rKgdj5blylU, we can see the commercial metrics is available for you at an instance level. This is however, not in our case and we cannot see it at instance level. This metric is important for us for cross charging. Due to this, we are not able to get charges under the tags which we have done for Cross charging.

From the recording:

How we see from our tenant:

Screenshot from Cross charging by tags application:

How can we see the commercial metrics from the instance level?

 

Thanks,

Erwin

entry.title: Re: Enabling the Onboarding Journey: Onboarding 2.0 in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello @shrutithakkar ,

We’re currently in the process of implementing ONB 2.0, and I have a question regarding the dynamic groups setup.

While the overall concept is clear, I’m wondering why it's necessary to create yet another user group. In our current configuration, we already maintain both permission groups and recruiting groups. Adding a separate group - particularly for HR Admins - feels redundant and adds to our maintenance burden. It’s also a bit limiting that we can’t assign a role or re-use an existing group in this context.

Another challenge we’re facing is the 200-user cap. Since we have over 200 HR Admins, we’re forced to split them into two groups, which increases complexity and manual upkeep.

Are there any options to re-use existing permission groups, plans to increase the group size limit, or perhaps recommended best practices to manage this more efficiently?

Thanks in advance for your support!

Kind regards,
Roos🌹

entry.title: Deploying Fiori application on S4 side on premise edition from BAS
entry.content:
Dear Specs ! 

I 've an S4 System with Embedded BW in it. I've been asked to create a fiori application that can launch process chain from the fiori . 

I 've never been built this kind of application before so I've started some search and I've got a little confusion. 

Can somehow Fiori application been deployed S4 side so I can call Function modules directly or only Odata calls are working ?

Could you give me some guidance on how to make the first steps ?  Calling directly the function modules, deploying the app directly upon S4 on premise system ? 

Sorry I mainly see solutions that covers scenario for cloud but for me this is not the case I would like to use Fiori as I would use a dynpro program calling FM-s directly not calling HTTP requests if it is possible.

entry.title: Re: ValueHelp with parameters where the value help provid...
entry.content:
Hi Bogdan,

quite difficult to judge the root cause with the information given below. I recommend you to open an incident for this issue.

Thank you and best regards

Fabian

entry.title: Good Receipt Production Embedded EWM
entry.content:
Hello together,

I'm struggling with GR Production in Embedded EWM in the Process Advanced Production Integration.

If I open the Transaction /SCWM/GRPI and put in the Search Area my Manufactoring order I can't find my Manufacturing Order, there is no document available.

 

 

I have checked the Customizing, but seems to be ok for me

Can anyone explain me why? Thanks for you support!

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Learning H1 2025 Release: Key Highlights and Features
entry.content: Thank you Arko

entry.title: Re: Michal's tips: Application Interface Framework (AIF) 2.0 - monitoring existing IDOCs
entry.content:
Hi @MichalKrawczyk ,

Thanks for bringing up this, helped me in my AIF configuration as a beginner

I have a requirement where I need to create a inbound IDOC and trigger it in the system. Then I need to do post goods movement 101 in custom function module which is part of AIF Configuration. While sending IDOC using "IDOC_WRITE_AND_START_INBOUND" the material document no is also getting created. Now what can I do in AIF related custom FM.

PLease suggest me the correct way of doing this.

entry.title: Could not find service binding of type 'destination'.
entry.content:
I am a beginner in CAP and using destination, but I keep encountering errors. I have already configured the destination in Cockpit, and the connectivity test works fine. However, when I try to connect using code, I keep getting the following error. Could you please tell me what step is missing in this binding?

error message:
[2025-04-24T06:24:19.328Z] WARN (environment-accessor): Could not find service binding of type 'destination'. This might cause errors in other parts of the application.
[cds] - Error: Could not find service binding of type 'destination'.
at resolveServiceBinding (/home/user/projects/incident-management/node_modules/@sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity/dist/scp-cf/environment-accessor/service-bindings.js:86:19)
at serviceToken (/home/user/projects/incident-management/node_modules/@sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity/dist/scp-cf/token-accessor.js:27:77)
at getProviderServiceToken (/home/user/projects/incident-management/node_modules/@sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity/dist/scp-cf/destination/get-provider-token.js:12:61)
at Object.getAllDestinationsFromDestinationService (/home/user/projects/incident-management/node_modules/@sap-cloud-sdk/connectivity/dist/scp-cf/destination/destination-accessor.js:99:65)
at async ProcessorService.getInternalData (/home/user/projects/incident-management/srv/services.js:113:17)
at async next (/home/user/projects/incident-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:64:17)
at async ProcessorService.handle (/home/user/projects/incident-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:62:10)
at async ProcessorService.handle (/home/user/projects/incident-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/common/Service.js:81:16)
[error] - 500 > {
code: '500',
message: "Could not find service binding of type 'destination'."
}
 
code :
 
const destination = await connect.getDestination('test')
console.log("destination", destination);
const res1 = await httpClient.executeHttpRequest(destination);
console.log("after result", res1);
 
or using the mock destinations, still the same error message:
 
const reg = await connect.registerDestination({name: 'test', url: 'https://google.com'});
console.log(reg);
console.log('--------------------');
const all = await connect.getAllDestinationsFromDestinationService();
console.log(all);

entry.title: Re: Joule speaks ABAP!
entry.content: The service request has now been successful (previously it was simply cancelled). Our Public Demo System has Joule for ABAP Developer Capabilities enabled.

entry.title: SAP Mobile App development
entry.content: Hi everyone, I’m working on a cross‑platform mobile project and need guidance on SAP mobile app development. What’s the best way to structure my app using SAP BTP SDK versus the Mobile Development Kit (MDK)? Which approach offers the most flexibility and ease for enterprise‑grade app development?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to create hierarchy variables in Datas...
entry.content:
Thank you Michael.
But do you maybe know any possibility to show this hiearchy in the prompt that comes up when the dashboard opens up?

entry.title: Publish SAP DM data to MQTT Broker using trigger(Manage Subscription app)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Use Case: MQTT is sending data to SAP DM and after processing the data SAP DM need to send (publish) the response(Acknowledgement) back to MQTT. 

Issue: as we understood is that if we select message publishing option in trigger(Manage Subscription) Action tab, then DM won't allow you to call the PPD and send the output. so as per understanding we can only send constant value to MQTT. 

Help Portal Link - Create a Subscription | SAP Help Portal

Ask: we need to send data to MQTT Topic from DM

can anyone please help us that how we can overcome this challenge to publish data to the MQTT topic from SAP DM.

Quick response will be appreciated.

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: Re: calling SAPF124 to z program
entry.content:
or pass directly the values to SAPF124 without using LT_SELSCREEN:

submit SAPF124  WITH x_shblf = 'X'
                WITH BUKRX = p_comp   " Company Code ('BFL')
                WITH GJAHX = p_gjahr  " Year ('2011')
                exporting list to memory
                and return.
*submit SAPF124  WITH SELECTION-TABLE lt_selscreen with x_shblf = 'X'

entry.title: What is going to replace req._query/req._queryOptions
entry.content:
Hi,

Since CDS version 8, req._query is not available anymore and is replaced by req._queryOptions. But, in the logs, it is mentioning that req._queryOptions is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. What else is going to be available to retrieve plain text OData parameters ?

entry.title: Re: Choosing the Right Authorization Method in SAP Integr...
entry.content:
Thank you for the quick response. I have three questions regarding this:

1. If I create a service key for OAuth authentication, can I use the ClientID and Client Secret for Basic Authentication as well? Can I disable this option?

2. Do I always need to share the .pem and .key files with external partners when using Client Certificate?

3. When connecting SOAP services via SOAMANAGER to the Integration Suite, do I only have the option to authenticate using Basic Authentication and Client Certificate? Is OAuth not supported here?

entry.title: Re: APIs for creating ticket and getting ticket details
entry.content: Can you tell me the process of doing this?

entry.title: Optional Sandbox system starter price
entry.content:
Hello, we use SAP S4H Public cloud edition 3SL. We are already in the Production tenant and the starter system was decommissioned after 30 days. We however need the starter environment again. 

I found that there is an option to subscribe to the optional Sandbox system for a fee. Could you please guide me on how to do it? And where can I find the fee amount? 

Many thanks

entry.title: Re: Could not find service binding of type 'destination'.
entry.content:
Hi,

This usually means your application is not correctly bound to the Destination service on BTP.  Please ensurepackage.json has an entry for the destination "test" in the following under cds.requires

"cds": {
  "requires": {
    "test-destination": {
      "kind": "rest",
      "credentials": {
        "destination": "test"
      }
    }
  }
}

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Learning Strategies for Complex Groovy Scripts and Gr...
entry.content: Thank you for the response. But it's possible to recreate the functions from the B2B Add-On for graphical mapping, right? I believe that in the EDI area, most mapping requirements can be covered this way, can't they?

entry.title: Re: How and where to see people bio and contact details in Community 2024?
entry.content: Please add a feedback if you can't see the bio or contact details of other people, or add a feeback if you can, thank you 😉

entry.title: Re: The SAP BW Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @nandini1601 ,

as of now SAP will do the configuration via note 3590400. This note is only about the configuration, which will soon also be automated. The operation of the BW DPG itself will be customer (BW Expert) driven.

Best Regards

Christof

entry.title: Re: Compensation - Promotion columns are not populate in worksheet after 1H 2025 Release
entry.content: Hi @EfratAssaraf, If the mapping for the promotions columns are all correct (https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/2zNkyVE?rc=10&doi=SAP949258) and this is something which was working previously, then I would recommend logging a support ticket to review. Thanks, Andres

entry.title: Re: Learning Strategies for Complex Groovy Scripts and Gr...
entry.content: Thank you for the links. Do you know if there are any courses focused on learning graphical mapping, Groovy Script, and XSLT?

entry.title: Re: Story Report for Goal Management - how many employees...
entry.content:
Emp with one Goal

IF(ISNULL([Goal Management#Primary Goal Fields#Goal ID]) ,0 ,1 )

 

GOAL Status

IF(ISNULL([Goal Management#Primary Goal Fields#Goal ID]), 0, 1 )

entry.title: Re: Configurable New Hire in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Good Day Andrew,

I know a lot has changed since this article was first posted. We are now at the point where we can customise the RNHD and PDC templates/screens. Including standard and custom MDFs. 

However, it seems when rehiring with old/new employment, any custom MDFs are not available during the RNHD step. It also seems SAP is not planning on introducing this any time soon. Our client does not wish to make use of the ADC step as this cannot be maintained in EC so that is not an option or a solution to this current situation. 

Do you have any suggestions or alternative workarounds for how we can get a custom MDF object/portlet which has been added to the People Profile to display during rehire scenarios as this MDF is crucial to the client's business.

Many thanks and hope to hear from you soon. 

Caitlin

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Graphical View Filter WHERE IN
entry.content:
Oh nice, indeed it works like that:
ID NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT ID FROM TABLE)

entry.title: Re: What's the best practice to update story reports to n...
entry.content: yes we also use this and it help to change the template and also some filter in section.

entry.title: Re: Joule with SFSF – Common Setup Issues
entry.content:
One to add to the list: SF sales demo systems do not allow changing IAS tenant. As a result, you need to align with the system admin of your S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition TDD if you plan on using it across SF and S/4. The only option is to change the IAS tenant used by S/4, or to purchase an additional Joule TDD tenant with the limitation of not being able to demo cross-product functionality.

KBA 2944990: “This upgrade process does not work for Sales Demos at present. Paid Sales Demos are created with pre-integrated IAS & IPS”.

entry.title: How to know if the element is an OData entity
entry.content:
Dear community,

Is there any way to confirm if the field is accessible with the OData APIs and, if it is, which OData API service to use to access it?

For example, how do you know whether this field "Goods Issue Date" is available for some OData API service?

Also, how do you find an accessible API to the field?

I would appereciate any small information on this!

Thanks&Regards,

is there anyone who can share a EMIGALL Document with me. 

All the Links in the Post are expired. It would be really helpfull if anyone can share a Guidline/Cook Book or any kind of Document with me.

Thanks and Kind Regards

entry.title: Re: Good Receipt Production Embedded EWM
entry.content:
Hi @akira887 ,

Please GR directly in RF (No EGR required) since you are using 

Embedded EWM...and let us know if any questions.
 
Kind Regards,
Ajit

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @berndstrecker ,

The multilevel advanced intercompany sales process looks really good and is precisely what we're looking for. However, it seems like it's on the roadmap for Q1 2026 for Private Cloud only? Do you know if/when it's coming to S4/HANA Public Cloud as well?

We are currently struggling with implementing S4/HANA Public Cloud due to the lack of the multilevel intercompany best practice. What would you advise in the meantime to get a similar result in Public Cloud?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Flatten Parent-Child Hierarchy into Level Hierarchy using HANA (2.0 & above) Hierarchy Functions
entry.content:
Hi @Abhishek_Hazra,

thank you for this. I followed your method to create the flat hierarchy. i run a hierarchy with about 2500 rows. It works well and fast.

However, i face some issues with hierarchy with more rows. I have another hierarchy which contains about 7828 rows. The source hierarchy table contains 3 columns, nodeID, parentID and nodeName.

after running it for 30 mins, i get the following error messages. We have 200GB for statement memory limit in the system. Do you any idea how to solve this problem? It would be very great if you could give some tips.

Thank you and best regards
chy

entry.title: Re: CPI to CPI Connection and both tenants will be on different subaccounts at different regions.
entry.content:
Hi @robertot4s ,

I am unclear about this — could you please clarify which adapter is suitable for connecting both CPI systems?

Thanks

Yogita

entry.title: Re: Could not find service binding of type 'destination'.
entry.content: Thank you, very much!

entry.title: Re: Could not find service binding of type 'destination'.
entry.content: Thank you, very much! This code still shows the same error both locally and in WebIDE, even after adding all the configurations. I want to understand if this binding needs to be handled manually. In my understanding, since I've already added the destination in the SAP BTP Cockpit, it should work directly in WebIDE. It feels like there's a missing step in the destination setup process.

entry.title: Re: S/4hana Public Cloud Exchange Rate API
entry.content: Not all the customer bought the solution of SAP Market Rate Management, how to handle this situation?

entry.title: Update custom field created in EKKO via CFL App while creating PO using BAPI_PO_CREATE1
entry.content:
Step 1  Extend  structures  bapi_te_mepoheader and  bapi_te_mepoheaderx using custom append structure for each of them and fill with the required custom fields.

for custom fields in the structure  bapi_te_mepoheaderx the type should be char1(bapiupdate)

Step 2 Fill the bapi extension in table as show below

 DATA :  ls_bapiextension TYPE bapiparex,
         lt_bapiextension TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiparex,
         ls_mepo_header   TYPE bapi_te_mepoheader,       
         ls_mepo_headerx  TYPE bapi_te_mepoheaderx.
 
      ls_mepo_header-po_number         = ' '.
      ls_mepo_header-zz1_wf_pdh =  <ls_data>-wf_id.
      ls_bapiextension-structure = 'BAPI_TE_MEPOHEADER'.
      ls_bapiextension-valuepart1 = ls_mepo_header.
      APPEND ls_bapiextension TO lt_bapiextension.
      ls_mepo_headerx-po_number         = ' '.
      ls_mepo_headerx-zz1_wf_pdh =  abap_true.
      ls_bapiextension-structure = 'BAPI_TE_MEPOHEADERX'.
      ls_bapiextension-valuepart1 = ls_mepo_headerx.
      APPEND ls_bapiextension TO lt_bapiextension.

Step 3 Create implementation for enhancement spot ES_BADI_ME_BAPI and implement the badi ME_BAPI_PO_CUST

Below is the code snippet for implementing the method ExtensionIN of BADI ME_BAPI_PO_CUST

METHOD if_ex_me_bapi_po_create_02~extensionin.
 
    DATA : lv_container TYPE me_max_container,
           lv_length    TYPE i VALUE 30,
           ls_poheader  TYPE bapi_te_mepoheader,
           ls_poheaderx TYPE bapi_te_mepoheaderx.
 
* get codepage handler
    DATA(lo_nls)   = cl_mmpur_bapi_po=>get_codepage_handler( ).
    DATA(lo_util)  = cl_mmpur_utilities=>get_instance( ).
 
* mapping for PO Header
    IF ( poheader IS SUPPLIED AND poheader IS NOT INITIAL )
      AND ( poheaderx IS SUPPLIED AND poheaderx IS NOT INITIAL ).
* get all relevant extension entries only for PO Header
      LOOP AT extensionin REFERENCE INTO DATA(lr_parex).
        CHECK lr_parex->structure EQ me->gc_poheader
           OR lr_parex->structure EQ me->gc_poheaderx.
        DATA(ls_parex) = lr_parex->*.
* start with container operation
        SHIFT ls_parex BY lv_length PLACES IN CHARACTER MODE.
        CLEAR lv_container.
        lv_container = ls_parex.
        TRY.
*convert container data to target structure
            IF lr_parex->structure EQ me->gc_poheader.
              lo_nls->cont_to_struc( EXPORTING cont   = lv_container
                                               langu  = sy-langu
                                     IMPORTING struc  = ls_poheader ).
              poheader-zz1_wf_pdh = ls_poheader-zz1_wf_pdh.
            ELSEIF lr_parex->structure EQ me->gc_poheaderx.
              lo_nls->cont_to_struc( EXPORTING cont   = lv_container
                                               langu  = sy-langu
                                     IMPORTING struc  = ls_poheaderx ).
           poheaderx-zz1_wf_pdh = ls_poheaderx-zz1_wf_pdh.
            ENDIF.
          CATCH cx_root.
* implement message handling in cause of failure (e.g. with MMPUR_BASE 051 or 052)
            lo_util->set_message_enaco_forced( ).
        ENDTRY.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements List report - display and upload images (part 1)
entry.content:
Hi @SumitKundu, 

thank you for the post! 😊

As we can see in your example, a separate call is made for each image displayed in the list. That can possibly result in heavy traffic to the SAP System. 

Is there a way to retrieve the images as part of the initial $batch request?

Thanks in advance.

Best regards,
Lukas

entry.title: Re: Setting up SAP Cloud Platform Transport Management for SAP Cloud Platform Integration
entry.content:
Hi Pieter,

I totally agree—Basic Authentication is not the most robust solution.

However, while it's still the only option available in NEO, Cloud Foundry offers extended authentication possibilities with CAS and cTMS. Here, you can use OAuthClientCredentials with a CAS instance at your target, which processes the content received from cTMS and forwards it to CPI.

Maybe this gives you a bit of perspective on the broader authentication capabilities beyond NEO. In this regard we also published this Tutorial lately, which guides you through the setup process.

Hope this helps,

Sven

entry.title: How to bulk upload data to a custom business object
entry.content:
Dear All.

I will create a custom business object to use as source data of Custom CDS View.

So, I want to add data(For Example : ID, TEXT) to a custom business object.

But I can't find that how to bulk upload data.

I understood that we can create Tile of Custom business object.

So, Please let me know that how to bulk upload data to a custom business object.
(by Tile or other methods)


Best Regards

entry.title: Re: FieldGlass integration for requisition creation
entry.content: Hi Josie, thanks for your reply. Does the ticket you mention replace the SSO config described here https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-employee-central/using-sap-fieldglass-with-employee-central/using-saml-single-sign-on-sso? This as this configuration does not work and that is also shown when you select the application name in provisioning "Fieldglass (Not Supported")

entry.title: Re: Lease Accounting - alternatives to Open Item Manageme...
entry.content:
Hi,

please see the SAP FAQ Note Note 2661237  point 16, which describes this requirement.

"...

16. Accounts: What do I have to consider when setting up the leasing accounts?

Via the clearing account(s), * to vendor account are split into a posting specific valuation. Examples for this can be found in the posting examples attached to SAP Note Note 2255555 .

The clearing account also enables you to post additional activities from different vendor posting sources depending on the valuation. This way you can post the clearing account for example from RERAPP, but also from appointment and recurring * purchase orders or invoice receipt 

Account determination of the clearing account:

SAP recommends the clearing account as a balance sheet account in the assets.
For the clearing account, open item management should NOT be activated (note that clearing of the account is not possible due to the ledger- / account-specific further processing. This applies for normal open item management as well as for ledger-specific clearing.
The clearing account line items should be managed.
For the clearing account, the indicator "Only Manage Balances in Local Currency" (SKB1-XSALH) must NOT be set.

Kind regards

Nils

entry.title: Re: FieldGlass integration for requisition creation
entry.content: Does Fieldglass not support oAuth authentication? Username/Password oData calls will not be supported by SuccessFactors for much longer. And what do you mean with API version, this is not something we can configure/decide in SuccessFactors?

I am following steps as per the tutorial. Is there any prerequisite to be able to get it working?

entry.title: Re: Automatic Batch Determination Issue in EWM (S/4HANA C...
entry.content:
Hi @JoseGuill ,

the batch determination in Warehouse Management is supported. It also considers the batch selection criteria if they are defined. In case this process does not work for you, I'd suggest to create an incident.

If you have already created an incident and received a negative response, please share the incident number with me and I can have a look.

Best regards,
Christian

②

However, the SmartTable still displays the input parameters PARAM1 and PARAM2 as table columns, instead of showing the actual data columns returned in the response. Do you have any idea how can I fix this?

entry.title: 提供開始：SAP Help Portal コンテンツを機械翻訳で即時に翻訳
entry.content:
SAP Help Portal に機械翻訳 (MT) 機能が導入されました。この新機能では、ドキュメントを即時に 39 の言語に翻訳できるため、任意の言語で必要な情報にアクセスすることができます。
※この記事は英語版の日本語翻訳です。併せてご覧ください。

使い方

  1. 任意の SAP Help Portal ページを開きます。
  2. ドロップダウンメニューから言語を選択します。
  3. コンテンツは、SAP Translation Hub の AI 機械翻訳を使用して、数秒で即時に翻訳されます。

フィードバックのお願い

お客様からのフィードバックは、コンテンツと翻訳のどちらを改善する上でも大変重要です。そのため、簡単にフィードバックをお寄せいただけるようにしました。

  • 各ドキュメントページの右側に、「このページは役に立ちましたか」というメッセージが表示されます。
  • 「いいえ」を選択すると、フィードバックがコンテンツの品質に関するものなのか、機械翻訳に関するものなのかを選択できます。
  • 「はい」を選択された場合、参考になった点をぜひお聞かせください。肯定的なフィードバックは、何がうまく機能しているかを弊社が理解する上で有用です。
  • 改善が必要な点について、フリーテキストコメントを追加いただくことができます。
  • SAP Help Portal にログインされている場合は、追加のコメントオプションが表示されます。電子メールアドレスを残すことで、コンテンツ作成者と改善点について直接ディスカッションすることもできます。

フィードバックへの対応について

いただいたフィードバックはコンテンツ作成者または言語エキスパートに直接送信され、必要な改善が行われます。これにより、ドキュメントの全体的な品質が向上し、より役立つものになります。

🌍今すぐ機械翻訳をお試しいただき、ご意見をお聞かせください。

🔗詳細について SAP Help Portal の新機能、SAP Translation Hub 文書翻訳 - 概要

entry.title: Sap pm dates
entry.content:
Hi, I’ve tried but I’m having trouble figuring it out because there are so many dates to choose from. I’m working in Power BI using data from SAP, which is retrieved from a Snowflake database. I want to get the same results as when searching in SAP. My question is: which dates (columns and table) does SAP use for the search?
First, I’m wondering about Notification (IW28) – which table and column does it look at for:

  1. Notification date – when I check the variant, it says DATUV (from date) and DATUB (to date).
    Next, I’m wondering about Work Order (IW38):

  2. From date (DATUV) to To date (DATUB)

entry.title: Re: Exporting Tables from Datasphere to Hana Datalake File System using Replication Flow
entry.content:
Hi Ravi,

First you need to create a CSR file using openssl. Post this, you need to get the CSR signed with Root certificate. Once done, use openssl to create p12 file (for Keystore file) and key file (for Keystore password).

Regards,

Karthik

entry.title: Material Description in Adobe Form not showing line breaks – All text appearing in single line
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I’m working on an Adobe Form for invoice printing, and I have a requirement related to the Material Description field inside the line items.

This field is a free text field — sometimes users maintain multiple lines of text using the Enter key (i.e., line breaks). For example:

 

Line 1
Line 2
Line 3
However, in the PDF output, all the text is appearing in a single line, like:
Line 1Line 2Line 3
 

My Requirement:

If the user presses Enter while maintaining the Material Description, I want the output in the PDF form to preserve those line breaks, so the result should appear like this:

Line 1
Line 2
Line 3

Request for Help:

Can anyone please suggest what changes I need to make in either:

  • The Adobe Form layout, or

  • The ABAP driver program

    to achieve this behavior?

    I want this to work dynamically — i.e., I don’t want to hardcode anything. The output should respect whatever line breaks are maintained in the source.

    Thanks in advance for your guidance! 🙏

entry.title: Understanding Test Data within SAP S/4HANA projects
entry.content:
The success of a SAP Testing project largely depends on how well your teams understand the organization of data in SAP and where to find the right data. This blog explores the architecture of SAP data and provides practical insights into effective test data management for S/4HANA projects.

Understanding Real-World Test Data Challenge

An SAP Materials Management (MM) team encountered significant issues while testing their procure-to-pay (P2P) process. They had created synthetic purchase orders manually for their testing activities.

Issues Faced:

  • The synthetic POs lacked historical goods receipt references, rendering them invalid for invoice verification (MIRO transaction)
  • The team used outdated vendor master data (LFA1), which resulted in blocked payments in the Financial module
  • The test data failed to include multiple tax codes, causing tax mismatches between purchase orders and invoices

Lessons Learned:

  • Transactional data must be realistic and comprehensive, including all necessary historical references
  • Using production-like purchase orders is essential for validating end-to-end processes
  • Test data provisioning should be automated whenever possible to eliminate manual errors

This example illustrates how incomplete or unrealistic test data can derail testing efforts and potentially lead to incorrect conclusions about system functionality.

Understanding The Architectural Framework of SAP Data

Before diving into test data strategies, let us understand how data is organized within SAP systems.

SAP data falls into three primary categories, each with distinct characteristics and functions:

1. Master Data

Master data represents the stable entities that form the foundation of your business operations. These are the core elements that remain relatively unchanged over time.

Key Characteristics:

  • Stored in persistent core tables
  • Used across multiple SAP modules to ensure consistency

Examples of Master Data:

  • Material Master (MARA): Contains all material-specific information used across purchasing, inventory, and production processes
  • Customer Master (KNA1): Stores essential customer details for sales and billing operations
  • Vendor Master (LFA1): Holds supplier information critical for procurement processes
  • Business Partner (BP000): Contains unified business partner records in S/4HANA

Where to Access Master Data:

  • via SAP Tables: MARA, KNA1, LFA1, BP000
  • via SAP Fiori Apps: "Manage Business Partners," "Display Material"
  • via Transaction Codes: SE16N, MM03, BP

2. Transactional Data

Transactional data captures the day-to-day business activities and operations like orders that constantly change as your business evolves.

Key Characteristics:

  • More dynamic and shorter-lived than master data
  • Drives real-time business processes
  • High volume, often requiring robust archiving strategies

Examples of Transactional Data:

  • Sales Orders (VBAK/VBAP): Header and item data generated during the order process
  • Purchase Orders (EKKO/EKPO): Header and item information created during procurement
  • Financial Documents (BKPF/BSEG): Accounting document headers and line items

Where to Access Transactional Data:

  • SAP Tables: VBAK, VBAP, BKPF, BSEG, EKKO, EKPO
  • SAP Fiori Apps: "Manage Sales Orders," "Display Purchase Orders"
  • Transaction Codes: VA03, ME23N

3. Configuration Data

Configuration data refers to the settings and parameters that define how the SAP system behaves and operates according to a business’s needs. For example, setting up a Sales Document Type (ZINT) for Internet Orders.

Key Characteristics:

  • Maintained through SPRO (SAP Project Reference Object)
  • Stored in customization tables
  • Configuration data is client-specific and is migrated or transported from one system (e.g., DEV) to another (e.g., QA, PROD) using transport requests.

Examples of Configuration Data:

  • Pricing Configuration (T685): Defines how prices are determined in Sales & Distribution
  • Material Types (T134): Categorizes materials (raw materials, finished goods, etc.)
  • Company Codes (T001): Defines organizational units in Finance
  • Account Determination (T030): Controls which GL accounts receive transaction postings

Where to Access Configuration Data:

  • SAP Tables: T685, T001, T030
  • SPRO Path: Various paths depending on the configuration area
  • Transaction Codes: SPRO, OBYC

Methods to Create and Manage SAP Test Data

Once you have a clear understanding of how data is structured, you can leverage the following tools or methods to create and manage test data effectively:

Where to look for Existing SAP Test Data

it's not always necessary to create test data for testing. In many cases, we can leverage existing data already present in the system. Let us explore various methods for identifying and reusing suitable data.

Transaction Codes (T-Codes)

Transaction codes provide direct access to master data records within SAP GUI. Below are some commonly used T-codes:

  • PA20 – Display HR Master Data: View detailed employee master data across various infotypes (read-only).
  • FI03 – Manage Banks: Display bank master data after it is imported into SAP.
  • MM03 – Material Master: Access material master records.
  • MD04 – Master Data Overview: View real-time master data and stock levels.
  • VA03 – Sales Order Display: Retrieve sales order details.
  • FB05 – Financial Accounting: View financial accounting documents.

SAP Fiori Apps

SAP Fiori provides modern web-based applications to manage and display master data. Some useful apps include:

  • Manage Product Master Data (F1602): Search, view, edit, create, and copy product master data.
  • Manage G/L Account Master Data: Access G/L account details via the SAP Fiori launchpad.
  • Manage Banks: View and update bank master data.

SAP Tables

The following SAP tables in SE16/ SE11 can help with master data

  • MARA (Material Master)
  • KNA1 (Customer Master)
  • LFA1 (Vendor Master)
  • BKPF (Accounting Document Header)
  • VBAK – Sales Order Header
  • AFKO – Production Order Header

SAP Reports and Queries

  • You may use SAP Query (SQVI/ SQ01) to generate reports on master data.

You may also leverage standard SAP reports, for example, MB52, for material stock overview

Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Test Data Management

  • Begin Early: Start planning your test data strategy during the test design phase
  • Automate Where Possible: Use automation tools for data creation, masking, and subsetting
  • Document As Much As Possible: Maintain comprehensive documentation of test data

entry.title: Re: Good Receipt Production Embedded EWM
entry.content: Can you share screeen shots, pmr, production otder, rf error please.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Wien - 08.05.2025
entry.content:
Ein gemütlicher Abend in lockerer Runde - zum zwanglosen Austausch über Themen rund um SAP und mehr.
Von der SAP Community, für die SAP Community.

Tritt der Gruppe Wien bei und subscribe den Blog-Post SAP Stammtisch Wien um über zukünftige Events informiert zu werden.

Bitte um verlässliche Zusage mit der RSVP Option rechts - das erleichtert uns die Reservierung in den jeweiligen Lokalen sehr.

entry.title: RFQ Contracts PO Release Strategy Setup
entry.content:
Setup Release Strategy for External Documents

External documents release strategy is always to be set as header wise with selection of header wise tables and fields. The header table to be referred is CEKKO. The same class is to be used for Objects of the external procurement. 

 

Create Characteristics CT04: 

Triggering this Release Strategy Based upon Document Types only, however user can create characteristics based upon the requirement.  

 

Create Class – Cl02

 

Enter the characteristics in the class.

 

Release Strategy setup for RFQ

SPRO Path - SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> RFQ and Quotation (SAP ERP) -> Release Procedure for RFQs -> Define Release Procedure for RFQs

 

Setup Release Group:

Setup Release Codes -

Setup Indicators:

Setup Release Strategy:  

 

Release Prerequisites:

Classification:

 

Simulate Release:

 

Assign object to classes - Cl20N

Transaction– ME41 RFQ Creation

ME45 RFQ release -

 

Setup Release Strategy for Purchase Orders.

SPRO Path - SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Purchase Order -> > Release Procedure for Purchase Orders -> Define Release Procedure for Purchase Orders

Create a New Release Group for PO with the same defined class used for RFQ.

 

Transaction – Po Creation

Setup Release Strategy for Contracts.

SPRO Path - SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Materials Management -> Purchasing -> Contract-> Release Procedure for Contracts -> Define Release Procedure for Contracts

 

 

Transaction: Create Contract. 

Transaction: ME35K (Release Contracts) 

Regards,

Sagnik Chakravarty.

entry.title: Re: Sap pm dates
entry.content:
Hi,

For IW28 (Notifications), SAP filters by creation date ERDAT from table QMEL.
For IW38 (Work Orders), it filters by basic start date GSTRP from table AFKO.
Use these fields in Power BI to match SAP's DATUV/DATUB filtering.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Create BP in S4 through ECC CREMAS05 - BUT000-BPEXT
entry.content: Thanks for the information on that, while digging a bit into that i found method FILL_AND_MAINTAIN_BP_FROM_VEND where "CALL BADI lr_badi_extension->map_external_data" is called. In there i was able to find the structure/fields which need to be filled "CT_DATA-PARTNER-CENTRAL_DATA-COMMON-DATA-BP_CENTRALDATA-PARTNEREXTERNAL"

entry.title: Re: Sap pm dates
entry.content:
Hi,

For IW28 (Notifications), SAP filters by creation date (ERDAT) from table QMEL.
For IW38 (Work Orders), it filters by basic start date (GSTRP) from table AFKO.
Use these fields in Power BI to match SAP's DATUV/DATUB filtering.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Free SAP Certification and practice systems for students & lecturers
entry.content:
Hi @hotiendat117 

I’m sorry to hear that you're still experiencing issues with the activation email.

I can confirm that your account has been provisioned. You should have received an activation email in your mailbox by now. Please make sure to check your spam or junk folders just in case it was filtered there.

If you still can’t locate the email, let me know and I’ll be happy to assist further.

Looking forward to your confirmation.

Best regards,

Maria

entry.title: Purchase Order CO commitment inconsistencies
entry.content:
Dear all,

We have an issue reporting figures from one PS project. There are some CO documents posted with value zero at some Purchase Order documents on Commitment Documents level while I can see the amount at the Good Receipt CO document. 

It seems there is a discrepancy between the CO tables totals and positions so I executed SAP note 1746429. It did not change the difference between PS and FM reports on the project.

Please help me on this issue that affects the delivery of Donors reports. 

Best,

Rodolfo

entry.title: SAP DM - Roles, Spaces & Groups
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we have created roles with the role templates in BTP. Is there any possibility to delete specific apps from roles or do we need to accept that a user/user group has all apps included in a roll template?

We also asked ourselves if it is possible to create and edit Spaces & Groups in DM for whole user groups?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: CO Configuration Error in OKZ3 (Default Values for Or...
entry.content:
Hi,

Also check configuration in 0PL8.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Quarterly Release Highlights: SAP BTP Security and Identity & Access Management
entry.content:
Hi Wallace, 

thank you for your comment!

We publish planned features and enhancements in the SAP Roadmap Explorer, which is the ultimate source of information for new capabilities or improvements. Release Notes contain information about newly released features. Other sources, such as this blog, serve the purpose of putting a spotlight on selected new developments.

I hope this answers your question - 

Kind regards
Regine

entry.title: Re: Trust Configuration" in SAP BTP Trial Account – Unable to Proceed with SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content: Why do you need Trust Configuration to enable Work Zone?

entry.title: SAP Process Improvement Suggestion – Payment Received at One Location, Material Sales at Multiple Lo
entry.content:
SAP Process Improvement Suggestion – Payment Received at One Location, Material Sales at Multiple Locations

In our current SAP setup, when a payment is received at a single location but the sales are made from multiple locations, the accounts team needs to manually split the payment and book multiple transactions based on the respective sales locations. This leads to extra effort, potential errors, and inefficiencies in reconciliation.

Suggested Improvement:
Introduce a "Single Click Payment Split by Location" functionality in SAP, where:

  • The system automatically identifies the sales locations related to the payment.

  • It enables the accounts team to split and assign the received amount location-wise with just one action.

  • This ensures accurate financial reporting, faster processing, and reduced manual intervention.

 Benefits:

  • Simplifies the workflow for the accounts team

  • Improves data accuracy and audit trail

  • Enhances visibility of payment allocation across locations

Would love to hear thoughts from fellow SAP users or consultants – has anyone implemented something similar?
